name: Table of Tales: The Crooked Crown

reviews.review_description: It's of the same vein as other grid tactics games like Demeo, but more approachable and with a story. There's even lots of moments where your decisions affect the direction the story takes. A concept I wish we would see more of in other games. I also love the way the board is animated with each hex rising and falling. But sometimes the game feels inconsistently balanced.  Whether intentional or bugged, a few encounters had almost no opposition and enemies would just kinda loiter around letting you tackle the objectives freely. This standing in stark contrast to other encounters where its a desperate fight through unending swarms.  A neat game worth playing, but not perfectly executed.
Fun game, great presentation. Cozy world brought to life.

This is one of the games that I always go back to. It's quite relaxing and the narrative makes me go for that one more chapter. Definitely not as hard as Demeo's gameplay but challenging enough for one to think through the skills available. I wish there were more Tales in the future. 😊

After standing playing multiple titles, this is my go to… to end my play session. A seated narrative adventure mixing elements of RPG, D&D, chance, multiple decisions that affect your adventure. A great journey with a dundeon master who narrates your play. A hidden gem. Worth the 20$ bucks but I got it on sale and highly recommend!

So first the bad: there is some minor glitching like two ships overlaying each other. And it is very short (I beat it in like 3 hours)

Now the Good: it is amazing, the story telling us everything and I was so deep in the story the ending felt beautiful almost 


To developers: pull a demeo and give us more stories please I would happily get more and play over and over this was amazing.

Last time I played this I enjoyed it and wasn't forced to use hand tracking.  Now when I try it, it won't even let me open it unless hand tracking is enabled. I don't want to use glitchy, slow, cumbersome controls. Way to spoil a game.

As others have already mentioned, where being prompted about using handtracking on the Quest 3. Handtracking in general doesn't work that well for me and I have it turned of globally because it annoys me. But now I must have it turned on in order to start - post starting you can switch to your controllers - the game.

The game itself is fun but this is a major issue which seems to be easily solvable. I'm clueless why the developer doesn't gives us an update for it.

Other than that I'm really missing a procedural generated dungeon mode. The story is fun and a great feature, but it would have been a nice addition and upped the reply-value. Perhaps the developer can make a DLC for this!

reviews.review_title: If  you like Demeo you will like this...
reviews.review_description:
...and I do!
Great game, doesn't have Demeo's multiplayer killer feature but the story is good. As many others have pointed out, the hand tracking is annoying but you can play it with controllers as well, so no big deal. It does insist that you use hand tracking when you first start it but never again.
Would give it 5 stars... But being forced to turn on handtracking just to open the game... I had to give it only 1. Please disable this requirement and I will give you the other 4!

It's a very BAD IDEA to use the triggers to skip dialog.  And there is no way to get it back. I find myself skipping a lot of it and it makes the game unplayable.  Can't pick stuff up to look at it or If you put the controller down it skips. Stupidest thing I've seen in a game yet. I think it's time to return it if there is no response

NEEDS MULTIPLE SAVE GAME SLOTS. My Wife and I have to share a save? C’mon… We both want to get immersed in the story, but that’s not possible. Seems like a huge oversight to not include this common feature. Hopefully it’s quickly remedied. 2 stars for now’s

I am hooked on the story and gameplay; however, my neck aches as you cannot seem to adjust the camera angles or move around in game to make it more comfortable (i.e. like in demeo). Therefore I may not be able to play until this is adjusted.

Narrative driven turn based adventure that, whilst having a short campaign, is meant to be replayed as your choices change the direction of the story. The gameplay loop is similar to a board game using a card mechanic to attack/heal with different abilities. There are usually goals or activities you can do which are activated by rolling the dice and achieving a certain dice roll. Graphics are simple but there is a certain charm to watching your pieces bounce around the board. The story is narrated well enough and reminds me of Moss. It isn't exactly thought provoking or aims to hit high emotional marks but does a decent job of establishing all the heroes and driving the story forward at a good pace. Ultimately however it is only fairly short and can be completed in a few hours on the first playthrough so it's difficult to make the stakes high over such a time frame.

Would love to see the developer iterate and improve on the formula. Maybe add a coop feature similar to Demeo.

reviews.review_title: Kept from greatness by short length
reviews.review_description: The mechanics of this game are fairly cool very simple and if this was something that could be used multiplayer I could excuse the short single player game however it's limited on what it can do and the length of its campaign
Like most VR games, it got boring way too fast.

This is definitely different than Demeo. Demeo is a dungeon crawl and this is more like an animated choose your own adventure book/game. It’s easy to pick up and learn but looks like it will require a good bit of strategic thinking later on. The graphics are pretty cool i love the concept. Not sure how long it is because I’m only about an hour in so far.
A wonderful tabletop RPG in VR with an old school feeling. The dungeon master (the bird) is top notch. Thoroughly enjoyed every minute.

If you enjoy, dice, rolling, interesting characters, and vast room for imagination this game is the best way to spend a couple of hours

Having the hand tracking without having to use the controllers is the-bomb dot com 

Can’t wait to see what else comes out like this !!!

Gameplay-wise, the game provides just the right amount of challenge. You can do a single playthrough in under a day, but it's interesting to see how your choices and dice/battle outcomes affect progression so it's worth replaying and trying different things even after you beat the game once.

The graphics are charming. It's not super duper high resolution or anything like that, but everything is detailed enough and the art has a consistent style that I found endearing.

The narrator's voice acting was solid and fun to listen to.

Overall a fun game and worth the money.
A cross between xcom and d&d/hero quest.
